description: secretx-k8s/src/lib.rs:220-227. Comment at 222-225 claims 'SSA is supposed to create-or-update; a 404 here would mean the API group itself is unavailable rather than the resource being absent. Fall through to an explicit POST as a last resort.' This reasoning is wrong: (1) if the core/v1 API group is unavailable, a subsequent POST will also 404 — the fallthrough does nothing useful; (2) the realistic 404 from SSA is a missing namespace, in which case POST will also 404; (3) if SSA succeeds at creating but the response is misclassified somehow, the POST will 409 AlreadyExists and be mapped to Backend (see prior issue). Either remove the fallthrough (treat 404 from SSA as the original error and let it surface) or document a concrete reproducible scenario where the fallthrough actually recovers. Without that, this is unjustified complexity that hides bugs.
title: SSA 404 fallthrough to POST is dead code or actively harmful
